vulnerability.reason: Shoppers’ reliance on ranking reflecting relevance is semantically present as reliance on visual similarity and inability to detect textual bias
vulnerability.reference: Shoppers make purchase decisions based on top-ranked results, assuming ranking reflects relevance.
vulnerability.generated: Shoppers rely on the ranking reflecting visual similarity and cannot detect when it is biased toward textual semantics.

impact.reason: shoppers are affected, but damage types differ: financial loss and reduced trust become frustration and platform revenue loss
impact.reference: Consumers buy irrelevant or inferior products, causing financial loss and reduced trust in the platform.
impact.generated: Less relevant products appear higher, leading to wrong purchase decisions, frustration, and platform revenue loss.

dose_response.reason: biased hash codes causing less relevant products higher and wrong purchase decisions matches the ranking-inversion chain
dose_response.reference: Biased hash codes from external textual guidance cause binary Hamming distance to invert ranking, exposing e-commerce shoppers who trust top results to irrelevant products, leading to financial loss and reduced trust.
dose_response.generated: External textual guidance biasing hash codes toward noisy text semantics, through e-commerce image search, triggers shoppers' undetectable reliance on visual ranking, leading to less relevant products appearing higher, wrong purchase decisions, and revenue loss.
